etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 𡈼(𡈼) -> to present
etymology.interpretation: 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") suggests speaking or presenting, while 𡈼 provides the pronunciation. The character implies presenting something orally or making it known through speech.

parts.english: phonetic component with no independent meaning

examples.chinese: 他在会议上呈交了报告(他在會議上呈交了報告)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài huìyì shàng chéngjiāo le bàogào.
examples.english: He presented the report at the meeting.

examples.chinese: 天空呈现出晚霞的颜色(天空呈現出晚霞的顏色)。
examples.pinyin: Tiānkōng chéngxiàn chū wǎnxiá de yánsè.
examples.english: The sky presented the colors of the evening glow.

examples.chinese: 这幅画呈现出了一种宁静的气氛(這幅畫呈現出了一種寧靜的氣氛)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè fú huà chéngxiàn chūle yī zhǒng níngjìng de qìfēn.
examples.english: This painting presented a tranquil atmosphere.
